figures.caption: Fig. 4. Dynamic-only robustness at 400-byte budget. Dynamic-only embedding distortion vs delta packet drop probability p (10 Hz). Periodic keyframes (N = 9, 10) are compared with the adaptive algorithm (τh = 0.727, Nmax = 30), using 4 bytes/update and reliable keyframes. Lower is better.

Fig. 2. Dynamic-only rate-distortion (no packet loss). Bitrate vs dynamiconly embedding distortion (cosine distance between ground-truth and reconstructed codebook embeddings on positions that change) for 10 Hz token-state streaming. Curves compare periodic keyframes (N ∈ 9, 10, 12, 17, 21) and adaptive keyframes (τh ∈ 0.814, 0.757, 0.727); points are payload budgets B ∈ 100, 200, 400, 800 bytes (4 bytes/update). Lower is better.

Fig. 3. Dynamic-only robustness at 200-byte budget. Dynamic-only embedding distortion vs delta packet drop probability p (10 Hz). Periodic keyframes (N = 9, 10) are compared with the adaptive algorithm (τh = 0.727, Nmax = 30), using 4 bytes/update and reliable keyframes. Lower is better.

Fig. 6. World model utility vs bitrate (no packet loss). Dynamic-position nexttoken perplexity (10 Hz) for the adaptive algorithm (τh = 0.727, Nmax = 30) versus rate-matched periodic baselines (N = 9 near 200 bytes, N = 10 near 400 bytes). Points are budgets B ∈ {100, 200, 400, 800} bytes (4 bytes/update); lower is better.

Fig. 1. System architecture overview. Vehicle A tokenizes each frame into a discrete state zt (18×32, K=8192) and maintains a sender-side estimate z̃t−1. Keyframes are triggered by Hamming drift (τh, Nmax) and deltas transmit top-M cosine-ranked updates using codebook embeddings E under budget B; delta packets are dropped with probability p (keyframes reliable). Vehicle B reconstructs ẑt by applying deltas or resetting on keyframes.

Fig. 5. Adaptive keyframe behavior (no packet loss). Average keyframes per 20 s clip (10 Hz) versus Hamming drift threshold τh for delta payload budgets B ∈ {100, 200, 400, 800} bytes (4 bytes/update); larger B and higher τh yield fewer keyframes.
